user_query: I run a small flower boutique in San Francisco and keep my product list in a CSV. Please help me generate a simple catalog web page and a matching JSON feed for my site using the files in this workspace. Requirements: - Update config/site_config.yaml by setting tax_rate to 0.0875 and featured_category to "Seasonal" (leave other fields as-is). The outputs must reflect these values. - Implement scripts/generate_catalog.py to do all of the following: - Read products from input/products.csv. Include only rows where active is true (case-insensitive). Treat price as a decimal number and inventory as an integer. Split tags by ';' into a list. - Read shop settings from config/site_config.yaml. - Read the HTML template at templates/catalog_template.html and replace these placeholders: [[SHOP_NAME]], [[CITY]], [[CURRENCY]], [[TAX_RATE]], [[GENERATED_AT]] (ISO-8601 datetime), [[FEATURED_CATEGORY]], [[TABLE_ROWS]]. - For each included product, compute price_with_tax = round(price * (1 + tax_rate), 2). In the HTML table, show Price (with tax) formatted to two decimals; if currency is USD, prefix with a dollar sign. - Availability should be "In Stock" if inventory > 0, otherwise "Out of Stock". - Sort items first by category (ascending) and then by name (ascending). - Write two outputs: 1) output/catalog.html – the template with placeholders filled and [[TABLE_ROWS]] replaced with a row per included product in the order above: NameCategoryPrice (with tax)Availability. 2) output/catalog.json – an object with fields: shop_name (string), city (string), currency (string), tax_rate (number), featured_category (string), generated_at (ISO-8601 datetime), and items (array). Each item must include: id (string), name (string), category (string), base_price (number), price_with_tax (number, two-decimal rounding applied), inventory (integer), active (boolean), tags (array of strings), and in_stock (boolean: inventory > 0). - Run the script locally to generate both outputs using this command (arguments must be supported by your script): python3 scripts/generate_catalog.py --products input/products.csv --config config/site_config.yaml --template templates/catalog_template.html --out-html output/catalog.html --out-json output/catalog.json Input paths: input/products.csv, config/site_config.yaml, templates/catalog_template.html, scripts/generate_catalog.py (to be implemented/edited). Output paths to create: output/catalog.html and output/catalog.json. Success criteria: - The HTML shows only active products from the CSV, sorted by category then name, with correct In Stock/Out of Stock and USD prices including the updated 0.0875 tax rate. - The JSON includes the specified metadata fields, the updated tax_rate and featured_category values, and item entries consistent with the CSV and computations. - The code reads from all provided input files, uses the template placeholders as specified, and produces the two files at the exact output paths above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
